1.arabic: بسم الله الرحمن الرحيم ن ۚ والقلم وما يسطرون
1.english: Nun. By the pen and what they inscribe,
You are not, [O Muhammad], by the favor of your Lord, a madman.
And indeed, for you is a reward uninterrupted.
1.transliteration: Wa inna laka la ajran ghaira mamnoon

1.english: And indeed, you are of a great moral character.
So you will see and they will see
Which of you is the afflicted [by a devil].

1.arabic: إن ربك هو أعلم بمن ضل عن سبيله وهو أعلم بالمهتدين
1.english: Indeed, your Lord is most knowing of who has gone astray from His way, and He is most knowing of the [rightly] guided.
1.transliteration: Innaa Rabbaka Huwa a'lamu biman dalla 'an sabeelihee wa Huwa a'lamu bilmuhtadeen

1.english: Then do not obey the deniers.
They wish that you would soften [in your position], so they would soften [toward you].
And do not obey every worthless habitual swearer
1.transliteration: Wa laa tuti' kulla hallaa fim maheen

1.english: [And] scorner, going about with malicious gossip -
A preventer of good, transgressing and sinful,
Cruel, moreover, and an illegitimate pretender.
Because he is a possessor of wealth and children,

1.arabic: إذا تتلى عليه آياتنا قال أساطير الأولين
1.english: When Our verses are recited to him, he says, "Legends of the former peoples."
1.transliteration: Izaa tutlaa 'alaihi aayaatunaa qaala asaateerul awwaleen

1.english: We will brand him upon the snout.

1.arabic: إنا بلوناهم كما بلونا أصحاب الجنة إذ أقسموا ليصرمنها مصبحين
1.english: Indeed, We have tried them as We tried the companions of the garden, when they swore to cut its fruit in the [early] morning
1.transliteration: Innaa balawnaahum kamaa balawnaaa As-haabal jannati iz 'aqsamoo la-yasri munnahaa musbiheen

1.english: Without making exception.

1.arabic: فطاف عليها طائف من ربك وهم نائمون
1.english: So there came upon the garden an affliction from your Lord while they were asleep.
1.transliteration: Fataafa 'alaihaa taaa'i fum mir rabbika wa hum naaa'imoon

1.english: And it became as though reaped.
And they called one another at morning,

1.arabic: أن اغدوا على حرثكم إن كنتم صارمين
1.english: [Saying], "Go early to your crop if you would cut the fruit."
So they set out, while lowering their voices,

1.arabic: أن لا يدخلنها اليوم عليكم مسكين
1.english: [Saying], "There will surely not enter it today upon you [any] poor person."
And they went early in determination, [assuming themselves] able.
But when they saw it, they said, "Indeed, we are lost;
1.transliteration: Falammaa ra awhaa qaalooo innaa ladaaalloon

1.english: Rather, we have been deprived."

1.arabic: قال أوسطهم ألم أقل لكم لولا تسبحون
1.english: The most moderate of them said, "Did I not say to you, 'Why do you not exalt [Allah]?' "
1.transliteration: Qaala awsatuhum alam aqul lakum law laa tusabbihoon

1.arabic: قالوا سبحان ربنا إنا كنا ظالمين
1.english: They said, "Exalted is our Lord! Indeed, we were wrongdoers."
1.transliteration: Qaaloo subhaana rabbinaaa innaa kunnaa zaalimeen

1.english: Then they approached one another, blaming each other.

1.arabic: قالوا يا ويلنا إنا كنا طاغين
1.english: They said, "O woe to us; indeed we were transgressors.
1.transliteration: Qaaloo yaa wailanaaa innaa kunnaa taagheen

1.arabic: عسى ربنا أن يبدلنا خيرا منها إنا إلى ربنا راغبون
1.english: Perhaps our Lord will substitute for us [one] better than it. Indeed, we are toward our Lord desirous."
1.transliteration: 'Asaa rabbunaaa any yubdilanaa khairam minhaaa innaaa ilaa rabbinaa raaghiboon

1.arabic: كذلك العذاب ۖ ولعذاب الآخرة أكبر ۚ لو كانوا يعلمون
1.english: Such is the punishment [of this world]. And the punishment of the Hereafter is greater, if they only knew.
1.transliteration: Kazaalikal azaab, wa la'azaabul aakhirati akbar; law kaanoo ya'lamoon

1.arabic: إن للمتقين عند ربهم جنات النعيم
1.english: Indeed, for the righteous with their Lord are the Gardens of Pleasure.
Then will We treat the Muslims like the criminals?
What is [the matter] with you? How do you judge?
Or do you have a scripture in which you learn
That indeed for you is whatever you choose?

1.arabic: أم لكم أيمان علينا بالغة إلى يوم القيامة ۙ إن لكم لما تحكمون
1.english: Or do you have oaths [binding] upon Us, extending until the Day of Resurrection, that indeed for you is whatever you judge?
1.transliteration: Am lakum aymaanun 'alainaa baalighatun ilaa yawmil qiyaamati inna lakum lamaa tahkumoon

1.english: Ask them which of them, for that [claim], is responsible.

1.arabic: أم لهم شركاء فليأتوا بشركائهم إن كانوا صادقين
1.english: Or do they have partners? Then let them bring their partners, if they should be truthful.
1.transliteration: Am lahum shurakaaa'u falyaatoo bishurakaaa 'ihim in kaanoo saadiqeen

1.arabic: يوم يكشف عن ساق ويدعون إلى السجود فلا يستطيعون
1.english: The Day the shin will be uncovered and they are invited to prostration but the disbelievers will not be able,
1.transliteration: Yawma yukshafu 'am saaqinw wa yud'awna ilas sujoodi falaa yastatee'oon

1.arabic: خاشعة أبصارهم ترهقهم ذلة ۖ وقد كانوا يدعون إلى السجود وهم سالمون
1.english: Their eyes humbled, humiliation will cover them. And they used to be invited to prostration while they were sound.
1.transliteration: Khaashi'atan absaaruhum tarhaquhum zillatunw wa qad kaanoo yud'awna ilassujoodi wa hum saalimoon

1.arabic: فذرني ومن يكذب بهذا الحديث ۖ سنستدرجهم من حيث لا يعلمون
1.english: So leave Me, [O Muhammad], with [the matter of] whoever denies the Qur'an. We will progressively lead them [to punishment] from where they do not know.
1.transliteration: Fazarnee wa many yukazzibu bihaazal hadeesi sanastad rijuhum min haisu laa ya'lamoon

1.english: And I will give them time. Indeed, My plan is firm.
1.transliteration: Wa umlee lahum; inna kaidee mateen

1.arabic: أم تسألهم أجرا فهم من مغرم مثقلون
1.english: Or do you ask of them a payment, so they are by debt burdened down?
1.transliteration: Am tas'aluhum ajran fahum mim maghramim musqaloon

1.english: Or have they [knowledge of] the unseen, so they write [it] down?

1.arabic: فاصبر لحكم ربك ولا تكن كصاحب الحوت إذ نادى وهو مكظوم
1.english: Then be patient for the decision of your Lord, [O Muhammad], and be not like the companion of the fish when he called out while he was distressed.
1.transliteration: Fasbir lihkmi rabbika wa laa takun kasaahibil boot; iz naadaa wa huwa makzoom

1.arabic: لولا أن تداركه نعمة من ربه لنبذ بالعراء وهو مذموم
1.english: If not that a favor from his Lord overtook him, he would have been thrown onto the naked shore while he was censured.
1.transliteration: Law laaa an tadaara kahoo ni'matum mir rabbihee lanubiza bil'araaa'i wa huwa mazmoom

1.english: And his Lord chose him and made him of the righteous.

1.arabic: وإن يكاد الذين كفروا ليزلقونك بأبصارهم لما سمعوا الذكر ويقولون إنه لمجنون
1.english: And indeed, those who disbelieve would almost make you slip with their eyes when they hear the message, and they say, "Indeed, he is mad."
1.transliteration: Wa iny-yakaadul lazeena kafaroo la-yuzliqoonaka biabsaarihim lammaa saml'uz-Zikra wa yaqooloona innahoo lamajnoon

1.english: But it is not except a reminder to the worlds.
1.transliteration: Wa maa huwa illaa zikrul lil'aalameen
